2604.21511.title: From Tokens to Concepts: Leveraging SAE for SPLADE
2604.21511.paper_summary: Learned Sparse IR models, such as SPLADE, offer an excellent efficiency-effectiveness tradeoff. However, they rely on the underlying backbone vocabulary, which might hinder performance (polysemicity and synonymy) and pose a challenge for multi-lingual and multi-modal usages. To solve this limitation, we propose to replace the backbone vocabulary with a latent space of semantic concepts learned using Sparse Auto-Encoders (SAE). Throughout this paper, we study the compatibility of these 2 concepts, explore training approaches, and analyze the differences between our SAE-SPLADE model and traditional SPLADE models. Our experiments demonstrate that SAE-SPLADE achieves retrieval performance comparable to SPLADE on both in-domain and out-of-domain tasks while offering improved efficiency.
2604.21511.paper_summary_zh: 學習稀疏 IR 模型，如 SPLADE，提供了優秀的效率與效能權衡。<lb/>然而，它們依賴於基礎的詞彙庫，這可能會影響性能（多義性和同義性）並對多語言和多模態使用造成挑戰。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提議用使用稀疏自編碼器（SAE）學習的語義概念潛在空間來替換基礎詞彙庫。<lb/>在本文中，我們研究這兩個概念的兼容性，探索訓練方法，並分析我們的 SAE-SPLADE 模型與傳統 SPLADE 模型之間的差異。<lb/>我們的實驗表明，SAE-SPLADE 在領域內和領域外任務上的檢索性能與 SPLADE 相當，同時提供了更好的效率。
2604.21511.author: Yuxuan Zong et.al.
2604.21511.authors: Yuxuan Zong, Mathias Vast, Basile Van Cooten, Laure Soulier, Benjamin Piwowarski
